name: Cape Town
summary: Cape Town sits at the southern tip of Africa, watched over by flat-topped Table Mountain and lapped by two oceans. You can ride a spinning cable car up to the clouds, meet wild African penguins waddling on a beach, and visit the island where Nelson Mandela was once held prisoner.
funFacts: Table Mountain often wears a fluffy white cloud called the 'tablecloth' — a legend says it comes from a smoking contest between a pirate and the devil.
Cape Town belongs to the Cape Floral Kingdom, the smallest but richest of Earth's six plant kingdoms, with around 9,000 plant species, most found nowhere else.
About 2,000 to 3,000 African penguins live at Boulders Beach, waddling between giant granite rocks and braying like little donkeys at sunset.
Nelson Mandela was locked up on Robben Island for 18 years in a tiny cell barely bigger than a bathroom — today the prison is a museum run by former inmates.
South Africa has 11 official languages, so you might hear Xhosa clicks, Afrikaans, Zulu and English all spoken on the same busy street.
thingsToDo: Wander the V&A Waterfront right where your ship docks, watching seals splash, harbour boats bob, and street performers entertain shoppers near Table Mountain views.
Ride the spinning Table Mountain Cableway — the floor rotates 360 degrees so everyone gets a panoramic view of the city, ocean and Robben Island.
Tiptoe along the boardwalks at Boulders Beach in Simon's Town to watch wild African penguins nesting, swimming and sunbathing among smooth granite rocks.
Catch the ferry to Robben Island for a tour led by a former political prisoner, then peek inside Nelson Mandela's tiny prison cell.
Float through the towering kelp forest tank at the Two Oceans Aquarium, then meet rockhopper penguins, ragged-tooth sharks and a giant green sea turtle.
Explore Kirstenbosch Botanical Garden's Boomslang treetop walkway, hunting for proteas, sugarbirds and rock hyraxes (called 'dassies') along the slopes.

localWord.meaning: Hello (to one person) in Xhosa, one of South Africa's most-spoken languages

images.alt: Table Mountain rising over Cape Town's city bowl and harbour at golden hour.
Boats bob in the V&A Waterfront harbour with flat-topped Table Mountain behind.
African penguins waddle across the sandy boulders at Boulders Beach.
A row of brightly painted pink, blue, and yellow houses on a Bo-Kaap street.
Dramatic cliffs and blue ocean at Cape Point where two seas meet.
